full_text_rationale: Full text proposes SelfEvolve, a runtime code-generation architecture with a persistent knowledge base, functions repository, tool descriptors, and automated code reuse; accepted extensions are integrated for future requests and evaluated across 11 software self-extension tasks with baselines and ablation.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 013; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Md Asif Iqbal Fahim, Oluwadamilola Adebayo, Alessio Ferrari
titulo: Software Self-Extension with SelfEvolve: an Agentic Architecture for Runtime Code Generation
formato_armazenamento: Persistent knowledge base with a Prompts repository, executable Functions repository, JSON Tool Descriptors repository, accepted generated function code, generated tests, feedback from rejected iterations, and invocation-pattern updates.
framework_agente: SelfEvolve runtime self-extension architecture with Chat and Tool Dispatcher, high-level-to-low-level requirement generation, TDD code generation, intermediate adjudicator, and hot reloading
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; repeated-run baseline comparison; TDD ablation; category-level analysis
benchmarks_utilizados: 11 manually created software self-extension tasks across integration, compositional, and data-processing categories, each run five times.
metricas_desempenho: Pass@1, successful runs out of 55, average iterations, task-category pass rates
metricas_cl: Accepted functions are written to the Functions repository and tool descriptors/prompts are updated so later requests can reuse newly generated capabilities.
baseline_comparado: AgentCoder, AutoGen, MetaGPT, and SelfEvolve without TDD
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: The paper reports accepted generated functions, descriptors, and prompt updates being accumulated in the knowledge base but does not report total repository growth over all runs.
resultado_principal: SelfEvolve succeeds on 51 of 55 runs, or 92.7% Pass@1, across the 11 self-extension tasks.
desempenho_vs_baseline: SelfEvolve reaches 92.7% Pass@1 versus AutoGen at 30.9%, MetaGPT at 27.3%, and AgentCoder at 0.0%. Removing TDD reduces performance to 72.7% and raises average iterations from 2.2 to 4.7.
limitacoes: Evaluation uses only 11 manually designed tasks, one LLM/function-calling stack, and functional-correctness metrics; it does not establish regression guarantees for old functions or broader performance/security/maintainability properties.
ameacas_validade: Potential test-code collusion because the same LLM generates tests and code, small benchmark size, manually created tasks, OpenAI-specific function-calling interface, and limited evidence for multi-function or long-running deployment settings.
